title: Can quantum-mechanical description of physical reality be considered complete?
text:
Abstract
In a complete theory there is an element corresponding to each element of reality. A sufficient condition for the reality of a physical quantity is the possibility of predicting it with certainty, without disturbing the system. In quantum mechanics in the case of two physical quantities described by non-commuting operators, the knowledge of one precludes the knowledge of the other. Then either (1) the description of reality given by the wave function in quantum mechanics is not complete or (2) these two quantities cannot have simultaneous reality. Consideration of the problem of making predictions concerning a system on the basis of measurements made on another system that had previously interacted with it leads to the result that if (1) is false then (2) is also false. One is thus led to conclude that the description of reality as given by a wave function is not complete.

title: Álvaro Junquera
text:
Lab
  
  
    
     E-mail
  
  
      ORCID
  
  
      Google Scholar
  

  
  
I am a postdoctoral researcher at the Computational Social Sciences and Humanities Laboratory of the Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC). I’m part of the Welfare and Equity team, led by Prof. Caterina Calsamiglia.
I am interested in labour markets, public policy, and methods. My research is focused on the application of causal inference and machine learning to the analysis of labour market policies and industrial policies. I have taught data analysis courses using R.



07/11/25: Talk at the IV Workshop on International Trends in Economic Research (UGR) to present “More money, more effects?”.
05/11/25: Our project “Strategies for Human capital Advancement and Productive Employment” (SHAPE) has been awarded funding from the Ministry of Science, Innovation and Universities (Government of Spain).
28/10/25: The policy report on effective ALMP for people with mental disorders (written with D. Gallardo & D. Saeteros) has been published.
17/10/25: Our paper “From rules to forests” (written with C. Kern) has been published.

title: Personal
text: My greatest passion is the Carnival of Cádiz, a mixture of different arts with a deep concern for social problems and popular beauty. Every February, numerous groups share musical theatres ranging from the poetic to the comical.

title: Research
text: 👷 My research is driven by two big questions: (I) What explains the high unemployment rates in Spain? and (II) What interventions can reduce this problem?
